senses.definition: a secret reason behind a plan or action

examples.x: The politician's support for the new policy was questioned, as many believed he had a hidden agenda to benefit his financial backers.
The manager's sudden change in strategy raised concerns among employees, who suspected there was a hidden agenda behind the decision.
The company's CEO was suspected of having a hidden agenda when he unexpectedly laid off a significant number of employees.
The speaker's friendly demeanor masked a hidden agenda to manipulate the audience's opinions and gain personal advantages.
The rival team's unexpected cooperation had the team questioning whether there was a hidden agenda at play, aiming to undermine their success.

senses.definition: said to ask someone to keep a conversation secret

examples.x: Between you and me, I think our boss is planning to resign next month.
I heard a rumor, but it's strictly between you and me, so please don't share it with anyone else.
Between you and me, I don't think their relationship is going well, but they haven't told anyone yet.
I have a secret project in the works, but it's strictly between you and me until I'm ready to announce it.

word_list.pronunciation: b…Ļňąiňź√į …Ēňź…Ļ sňąe…™ …ź wňą…úňźd …źbňĆa ät ňĆ…õstňĆiňźňąe…™t É

senses.definition: to tell other people what is supposed to stay secret

examples.x: The detective warned the witness not to breathe a word about the ongoing investigation to anyone.
She promised her friend she wouldn't say a word about the surprise party they were planning.
He knew the consequences could be dire if he said a word about his involvement in the illegal scheme.
The employee was loyal to the company and wouldn't breathe a word about the upcoming product launch.
The children were instructed by their parents not to say a word about the surprise vacation they were going on.

senses.definition: used to refer to an agreement made in order to keep someone from sharing or revealing information that should be otherwise publically known

examples.x: The members of the organization formed a conspiracy of silence to protect their leader from the allegations of corruption.
The family maintained a conspiracy of silence regarding their relative's addiction, shielding the truth from outsiders.
The witnesses' conspiracy of silence hindered the investigation into the crime, making it difficult to gather evidence.
The employees' conspiracy of silence about the workplace harassment perpetuated a toxic environment.
The political elite engaged in a conspiracy of silence to conceal their involvement in the scandal, evading public scrutiny.

senses.definition: to not let something become revealed to others

examples.x: The prime minister is desperately trying to keep a lid on revelations from his former mistress's tell-all book.
No matter their efforts, the leaks kept coming and the organization never fully managed to keep a lid on the payment scandal.
Looking back, historians will remark how remarkably the administration had kept a lid on early warnings about the impending crisis.

senses.definition: to refrain from revealing one's true intentions or plans

examples.x: The negotiator kept his cards close to his chest during the business deal, not revealing his true bottom line to the other party.
She played her cards close to her vest when discussing her career plans, not disclosing her intentions until she had a solid plan in place.
The detective held his cards close to his chest during the interrogation, carefully extracting information from the suspect without revealing his own knowledge.
The politician played his cards close to his chest during the campaign, strategically revealing his policies and plans at opportune moments.
The entrepreneur held her cards close to her vest when discussing her new invention, protecting her intellectual property until she secured proper patents.

word_list.pronunciation: kňąiňźp ňĆ…õstňĆiňźňąe…™t É ňĆ Ćnd…ö wňą Ćnz hňą√¶t

senses.definition: to avoid sharing a secret with others

examples.x: Before the surprise party, she asked everyone to keep it under their hat to ensure the birthday celebrant remained unaware.
The inventor shared a groundbreaking idea with a colleague, trusting them to keep it under their hat until the patent process was complete.
The detective shared crucial information with the witness, stressing the importance of keeping it under their hat to protect ongoing investigations.

senses.definition: to decide to remain silent about something, particularly out of fear of making a situation worse

examples.x: The boss of the failed company is keeping schtum about his role in the disaster.
Though EU Council and Commission officials have stayed schtum about Britain's economic woes, others across Europe have been more blunt.
If you want to avoid getting into trouble, just keep schtum.

senses.definition: said as a promise or request to not tell something to anyone else

examples.x: The children promised their parents that "mum is the word" and they wouldn't reveal the surprise vacation destination.

senses.definition: used when one says that one will not reveal secret information
something about a person's past that they do not want to reveal or mention because it is embarrassing or unpleasant

examples.x: She always appeared composed and confident, but little did others know she had a skeleton in the closet.

senses.definition: used to refer to a room, in which a group of people knows or witnesses something that others do not

examples.x: I don‚Äôt want anything repeated outside these four walls.
Now, this can't go beyond these four walls, but Janet told me that she's pregnant!
Now, remember, what we discuss here stays within these four walls; it's confidential.
I trust you with this information, but please keep it within these four walls.
We've shared our deepest secrets and fears within these four walls, creating a bond of trust and support.

word_list.pronunciation: tňą…õl tňąe…™lz ňĆa ä…ĺ…ôv a ätsňąa…™d skňąuňźl

senses.definition: to share information that is secret or private

examples.x: I trust you not to tell tales out of school about what happened during our private conversation.
It's important to maintain confidentiality and not tell tales outside school regarding the company's internal affairs.
She broke the code of friendship by telling tales out of school about her best friend's personal life.
Please respect the privacy of our family and don't tell tales out of school about our personal matters.

word_list.pronunciation: kňąiňźp wňą Ćnz ňąo än kňąa äns…ôl

senses.definition: to keep one's opinions, plans, or intentions secret

examples.x: She always keeps her own counsel at times like these.
Up until now, I have kept my own counsel about my reasons for leaving the Department of Justice.
He preferred to keep his own counsel when it came to making major life decisions, relying on his own instincts.
Despite the pressure to reveal her plans, she chose to keep her own counsel and quietly pursued her goals.
The wise old man was known for his ability to keep his own counsel and provide thoughtful advice when asked.

senses.definition: to not let other people know about something

examples.x: But that was no reason to think she'd go along with keeping their relationship dark.
He decided to keep his financial troubles dark and not share them with anyone.
The company's executives made a mutual agreement to keep the impending merger dark until the official announcement.
The politician was involved in a scandal but managed to keep it dark until it was uncovered by investigative journalists.

senses.definition: a person who can be trusted with one's secrets

examples.x: Whereat he smiled, but remained dumb as an oyster.
Despite repeated attempts to get him to talk, he remained as close as an oyster, revealing nothing about his involvement in the incident.
Whenever we asked her about her personal life, she would become as close as an oyster, deflecting our questions with a smile.
The new employee was dumb as an oyster when it came to understanding the company's procedures and policies.

senses.definition: to conceal or destroy evidence of one's activities or whereabouts to avoid detection or punishment

examples.x: Roberts covered his tracks by throwing the knife in the river.
After the theft, he carefully covered his tracks, leaving no evidence behind.
The spy meticulously covered her tracks to ensure she couldn't be traced back to her mission.
He deleted his browsing history and cleared his computer cache to cover his tracks.
The politician attempted to cover his tracks by altering official documents and erasing electronic records.

senses.definition: to try to conceal flaws or problems temporarily, without addressing or solving them properly

examples.x: Conversely, if you have been papering over the cracks of a relationship these same eclipses will seek them out.
The private finance initiative has not papered over the cracks, although it has lined plenty of pockets.
The company tried to paper over the cracks by offering small bonuses instead of addressing the underlying issues of low morale.
Instead of having an open discussion, they decided to paper over the cracks, pretending that everything was fine.
The government's attempt to introduce cosmetic changes to the policy was merely an effort to paper over the cracks without addressing the fundamental flaws.
The couple decided to go on a vacation to paper over the cracks in their deteriorating relationship, hoping that time away would fix their issues.

senses.definition: to keep something secret or confidential and not to reveal it to others

examples.x: But officials deny they've been keeping their proposals under wraps.
The celebrity couple managed to keep their wedding plans under wraps to avoid media attention.
The government is keeping the investigation findings under wraps to prevent panic among the public.

senses.definition: a piece of personal or private information that one refuses to share with anyone

examples.x: She refuses to talk about her childhood, saying it‚Äôs a trade secret.
My uncle always keeps his plans for the weekend a trade secret.
He won‚Äôt share how he stays so fit, calling it a trade secret.
She keeps her beauty routine a trade secret from her friends.
